name: Feleipe Franks
hs_athletic_background: AT FLORIDA:2019 (REDSHIRT JUNIOR): Appeared in three games as a redshirt-junior in 2019, seeing action against Miami (Aug. 24), UT Martin (Sept. 7) and Kentucky (Sept. 14), starting all three … Threw for 698 yards and five touchdowns, rushing for 68 yards and a score over the three games … Went 17-for-27 in the season opener against Miami, throwing for 254 yards and two TDs, also ran for a score on eight yards … Put together a strong showing against Tennessee-Martin, racking up 270 yards and two touchdowns through the air on 25-of-27 passing, while also running for 37 yards … Also played in the SEC opener at Kentucky, going 12-for-17 for 174 yards and a TD, with 23 rushing yards.2018 (REDSHIRT SOPHOMORE): Played in and started all 13 games at quarterback, throwing for 2,457 yards with 24 touchdowns and six interceptions on the season… Totaled 350 rushing yards on 110 carries and a team-high tying seven rushing touchdowns… Led the Gators to a 10-3 record and No. 6 final ranking in the USA Today Coaches’ Poll… Became the first Gators quarterback since John Brantley in 2010 to start every game throughout the entire season… Finished the season with 31 touchdowns accounted for, tied with Chris Leak’s 2004 season for the seventh-highest single-season total in school history… His seven rushing touchdowns left him one shy of tying Tim Tebow (2006) and Jimmy Fisher (1979) for the sixth-most rushing touchdowns by a Gators quarterback in a single season… Ranked tied for 32nd nationally with 24 touchdown passes, which was also the highest single-season total by a UF quarterback since Tebow threw 30 in 2008… Finished the season tied for 17th nationally in touchdown-interception ratio… Among players with 315-plus attempts, he tied for 14th… Since the start of 1996, Tebow (7.50 – 2008; 5.33 – 2007; 4.20 – 2009) is the only Gator with a better touchdown-interception ratio than Franks’ 4.00 (24 to 6) in 2018… One of 12 FBS quarterbacks to throw more than 20 touchdowns and less than seven interceptions… Became the fourth Gators QB since 1996 to accomplish that aforementioned feat, and Franks had the second-most attempts (322) in that bundle, only trailing Tebow’s 2007 season (350)… Registered the highest passing yardage total since Tebow threw for 2,895 yards in 2009… His four games of three-plus touchdown passes were the most such games since Tebow had four in 2008, and they were just one shy of UF quarterbacks’ combined total from the 50 games spanning 2014-17… His six 200-yard passing games are the most since Tebow’s seven in 2009… Capped his season with Offensive MVP honors in the Chick-fil-A Peach Bowl, as he joined Tebow as the only other Gators quarterback since the start of the 2000 season to record a passing touchdown and rushing touchdown in a bowl game.. Ended the 2018 season on a streak of 135 pass attempts without an interception (last INT vs. Georgia in 1st Quarter), which ranks as the fourth-longest streak in UF history…2017 (REDSHIRT FRESHMAN): Played in all 11 games and made eight starts at quarterback, throwing for 1,438 yards with nine touchdowns and eight interceptions on the season…  Became the first redshirt freshman to start a season opener at QB for the Gators since Kyle Morris in 1988… Recorded 212 yards passing (19-for-28) with two touchdowns and one interception against Tennessee… UF defeated UT with a 63-yard touchdown pass from Franks to Tyrie Cleveland on the final play of the game… That play marked the only time in school history that the Gators defeated an opponent with a touchdown on the final play of regulation… Antonio Callaway’s catch-and-run from Will Grier against the Vols in 2015, which was also a 63-yard pass play, gave the Gators’ a 28-27 victory that season… Florida ran the exact same play, “Train Ride Open, Big Bend In.” on both occasions… Recorded a 79-yard rush in the third quarter against Texas A&M on Oct. 14, which is the longest run by a UF quarterback in school history… Combined with Malik Zaire to throw for 256 passing yards at Missouri on Nov. 4, which was a season-high for the Gators… In addition, that contest marked the first time that UF threw for at least 250 yards against an SEC opponent since Dec. 3, 2016 (2016 SEC Championship, Alabama)… Became just the second UF quarterback since 2012 to start at least eight games in a season… Totaled a 3-5 record as a starter…2016 (FRESHMAN): Did not appear in a game and redshirted.PRIOR TO FLORIDA:Participated in the 2016 US Army All-American Bowl in San Antonio, Texas… 2015 Elite 11 Finalist (was on the final squad of eleven and was the first invited to the competition)… Participated in Nike’s The Opening 2015 in Beaverton, Oregon… Named Class 5A Second Team All-State as both a junior and senior… Led his team to a 13-2 record as a senior and reached the Class 5A State Championship Game… Senior Season: Threw for 2766 yards with 35 touchdowns; also had 28 carries for 245 yards and four touchdowns, was the kicker for all kickoffs and field goals, along with most extra points and punts, made 90% (43-47) of his extra points and four of seven field goals, including a long of 47 yards, his longest punt was 68 yards and 44 of his 74 kickoffs were touchbacks… Junior Season: Threw for 2249 yards with 28 touchdowns; also had 28 carries for 425 yards and three touchdowns, was the kicker and punter as well, having 60 of his 74 kickoffs be touchbacks, seven of his 22 punts be inside the 20, 46 of 50 PAT attempts made, and six for seven on field goals with a long of 39 yards… Sophomore Season: Threw for 1842 yards with 18 touchdowns; also ran four times for 36 yards and a touchdown, handled most of the team’s kicking and punting duties, with nine of his 24 punts being touchbacks, 23 of his 31 extra points being made, and four of five of his field goals being made with a long of 33 yards… Played for Coach Barry Klees… Brother, Jordan, played wide receiver and signed with UCF in 2014… Also played baseball in high school.2019 MLB DRAFT:Franks was selected by the Boston Red Sox with the 947th pick in the 31st round of the 2019 MLB Amateur Draft… “I’m flattered that the Red Sox picked me this afternoon,” Franks said on June 5, 2019 following his selection. “However, I’m living out my dream being the quarterback of the Florida Gators. We’re all working extremely hard this offseason and I cannot wait to hit the field this fall.”… Franks hasn’t played baseball since his junior year of high school because he graduated in Dec. 2015 and early enrolled at Florida, was rated as the No. 500 overall baseball prospect in the country by Perfect Game in the spring of 2016… In addition, Franks was rated as the No. 117 overall player in the state of Florida and the No. 41 right-handed pitcher nationally… Franks was the first UF quarterback selected in the MLB Amateur Draft since Jeff Driskel was selected in the 29th round by the Red Sox in 2013.

name: Holton Ahlers
hs_athletic_background: 2021: Started all 12 games during junior campaign … Completed 256-of-414 passes (61.8 percent) for 3,126 yards … Threw 18 touchdowns with 10 interceptions … Stood among the nation’s Top 30 in passing yards per game (19th/260.5), completions (21st), total offense yards per game (27th) and passing yards (29th) … On the ground, rushed for 202 yards and six scores on 116 carries (1.7 ypg) … Caught a pair of passes for 27 yards (13.5 ypc) with one touchdown at Marshall (9/18) … In all, tallied a unit-best 3,328 yards on 530 plays – an average of 277.3 per contest … Directed an offensive unit that stood 39th nationally in total offense (433.2) and 54th in scoring (29.7 ppg) … One of four team captains at the conclusion of the season for the third consecutive year … Served as a game captain against Appalachian State for the season opener in Charlotte … Passed for 300-plus yards in a game three times (368/Marshall, 313/Memphis, 405/Navy) … Also threw for 200 yards or more against Appalachian State (295), Charleston Southern (238), Tulane (288), UCF (219), Houston (278), South Florida (220) and Cincinnati (228) … Tossed multiple TDs in a game six times including a season-best three on twice (Temple and Navy) … Twelve of 18 TD passes went for 10 or more yards, nine for 20-plus and two for 60 or more yards (63/Appalachian State, 60/Charleston Southern) … Completed season-best 30 passes (48 attempts) with a pair of scores … Rushed for 178 yards (of his 202) over the last five games including a season-best 78 against South Florida … Became first ECU QB to catch a TD pass in a game since Shane Carden in 2014 St. Pete Bowl (at Marshall/27 yards on 9/18/21) ... First Pirate to throw, run and catch a TD in a game since James Summers in 2016 vs. UConn - span of 52 games - at Marshall (9/18/21) ... One of three AAC quarterbacks to pass, rush and catch a pass in a game this season (Desmond Ridder/CIN and Dillion Gabriel/UCF) ... One of four active FBS QBs during 2021 with 10,000 yards passing/1,000 yards rushing - career ... On the season has accounted for 24 scores (18 pass, 6 rush) and added first career receiving TD ... Named to 2021 NFLPA Collegiate Bowl Big Board Watch List ... Midseason Watch List for Davey O’Brien Award (10/19) ... Three-time AAC Honor Roll (Marshall 9/20, Temple 11/8, Navy/22) ... Two-time Manning Stars-of-the-Week (9/20, 11/22) ... Named to Davey O’Brien Awards Great 8 (9/20) ... Named College Football Performance Awards Honorable Mention (11/22) ... Third-Team Preseason All-AAC pick by Phil Steele’s College Football Preview ... Fourth-Team Preseason All-AAC selection by Athlon Sports.2020: Started all eight games played, missing only the Navy contest (COVID-19 protocol) … Completed 165-of-269 passes (61.3 percent) for 1,921 yards and 18 touchdowns while only getting picked off nine times … Ranked among the nation’s Top 30 in three aerial categories – 25th in passing TDs, 26th in completions per game (20.6) and 30th in passing yards per game (240.1) … On the ground, rushed for 109 yards and a score on 54 carries (2.0 ypg) … In all, racked up a unit-best 2,030 total yards on 323 plays – an average of 253.8 per contest … Directed an offense that turned in the program’s 10th-highest single-season scoring average (30.2 ppg) in school history (and tops since 2014) … Selected to the Manning Award and Wuerffel Trophy Watch Lists … Served as a two-time game captain (UCF, Georgia State) … Eclipsed 300 passing yards twice with those performances coming in successive outings at Tulsa (330) and against Tulane (season-high 351) … Also recorded 200 air yards or more against SMU (298), Georgia State (236), South Florida (222) and UCF (215), while adding 182 at Temple in road finale … Had streak of 14-straight games with 200-plus passing yards snapped at Cincinnati (9-of-20 for 87), which also marked his first-ever sub-100-yard outing as a starter … Tallied multiple TD throws in six contests with a season-best four vs. SMU at Dowdy-Ficklen Stadium after contributing three each against UCF, South Florida, Tulsa and Tulane, while tossing a pair at Temple … Fourteen (14) of the 18 scoring strikes covered 10+ yards, while three went 60 or more (75 vs. South Florida and Tulane, 60 vs. Temple) … Of the nine interceptions suffered, six came in just two tilts (three each vs. Georgia State and Cincinnati) … Established new personal bests with 38 completions against the Golden Hurricane and a 75-yard run vs. Temple on the road … Also turned in his top single-game rushing effort of the campaign with 70 yards (on six attempts) and scored only ground tally against Owls with a one-yard plunge to cap ECU’s 28-3 victory at Lincoln Financial Field … Ran for 55 yards on a season-high 12 carries in the opener vs. UCF and added 35 on six totes at South Florida to round out other notable rushing contests … Averaged 240.7 passing yards per game in seven American Athletic Conference appearances (136-of-219 for 1,685 yards and all 18 TDs) … Tossed 10 scoring passes, threw only one interception and turned in a 288.0 aerial clip in just three home contests (61-of-101, 864 yards), while netting a 211.4 average in five road tilts with eight picks and eight touchdowns … Enjoyed consistent early and late splits with 1,003 yards and nine TDs in first four games before logging 918 yards and an identical nine score total in the final four outings … Fared better in day games (256.5 pypg, 101-of-173 for 2,282 yards and 12 TDs) than at night (213.0 pypg, 64-of-96 for 639 yards and six TDs) … Turned in top passing numbers during month of October (262.7 pypg) in comparison to September (215.0) and November (229.5) … Was 73-of-104 (70.2%) with 30 first downs gained and seven touchdowns in all first down snaps, while booking a 62.3 clip on second down (48-of-77, 25 first downs, 3 TDs), a 51.9 success rate on third down (41-of-79, 29 first downs, 6 TDs) and a 33.3 ledger on fourth down (3-of-9, 1 first down, 2 TD) … Completed 29-of-56 passes (51.8%) on third-and-long, moving the chains on 18 occasions and accounting for four scores … Excluding sacks, averaged 7.3 yards per carry on 33 “true” rushes and picked up 10 first downs … Netted a team-best 11.9 yards per attempt on 10 second down rushes … Credited with his third career tackle on a solo stop (following an interception) at Temple … Also launched his first collegiate punt against the Owls, a 32-yarder that resulted in a touchback … A fourth-team preseason All-AAC selection by Athlon Sports.2019: Opened all 12 contests during sophomore campaign … Passed for 3,387 yards (264-of-442) and 21 touchdowns with only 10 interceptions … Completion percentage was 59.7 … On the national (FBS) level, ranked ninth in total offense (3,746 yards), 12th in passing yards per game (282.2), 17th in completions per game (22.0) and 21st in passing yards … Rushed for 359 yards and six TDs on 108 carries, an average of 3.3 per attempt … Established three single-game school records during the course of the season – passing yards (535 vs. Cincinnati), passing TDs (six vs. SMU) and most total yards (556 vs. Cincinnati) … Aerial effort vs. Bearcats also rated as the third-highest single-game passing performance on the FBS level in 2019 (606, 570 by Washington State’s Anthony Gordon) … Trailed only Shane Carden and Dominique Davis on ECU’s single-season lists for passing yards (4th), most completions (6th) and most total yards (4th) … Surpassed 200 passing yards on nine occasions (300/5, 400/2, 500/1) … Accounted for at least one TD throw in nine contests, two or more five times, three or more thrice and four or more twice … Earned American Athletic Conference Honor Roll inclusion following efforts vs. SMU (32-of-42, 498 yards, 6 TDs, 8-25 rushing) and Connecticut (34-of-50, 374 yards, 1 TD, 6-22 & 1 TD rushing) … In addition to record-breaking passing numbers (32-of-52, 535 yards), accounted for five touchdowns against Cincinnati (4 air, 1 ground) …  Top rushing outing was a 78-yard, two-TD performance on 10 carries vs. Gardner-Webb in home opener … Also added 60 yards on 14 attempts – which included a season-long jaunt of 36 – against William Mary before recording best AAC ground numbers at UCF (15-47, 1 TD) … Tallied other rushing scores vs. Navy, Cincinnati and Connecticut … Suffered only one lost fumble in 108 attempts (at NC State) … Enjoyed favorable conference & non-conference splits, averaging 326.6 passing yards per game in eight league tilts (196-of-319 for 2,612 yards and 17 TDs) compared to a 193.8 yards-per-game clip in four non-AAC matchups (68-of-123, 775 yards, 4 TDs) … Home/road numbers were nearly identical in six contests – 134-of-230 (1,694 yards/282.3) and 11 touchdowns at Dowdy-Ficklen Stadium compared to 130-of-212 (1,693 yards/282.2) and 10 TDs away from Greenville … Turned in strong finish, averaging 374.2 yards per game with 15 scoring tosses in final six outings (190.3 ypg, six TDs in first six) … Closed campaign as arguably one of the hottest quarterbacks nationally, completing 125-of-192 throws (65.1 percent) for 1,715 yards (428.8 ypg) with 14 touchdowns during the month of November, which included the final four contests vs. Cincinnati, SMU, Connecticut and Tulsa … Accounted for 56 pass completions (12 TDs) and seven rushes (1 TD) that covered 20-plus yards, including five of 50+ – 75 (C.J. Johnson/Cincinnati), 72 (Blake Proehl/Old Dominion), 68 (Jsi Hatfield/(SMU, 57 (Tyler Snead/SMU) and 53 (Deondre Farrier/Old Dominion) … A total of 124 (of 264 completions) were for 10 or more yards … Pass success rate on third down was 51 percent (68-of-133) that resulted in 45 first downs and nine TDs … Was 109-of-165 (66%) on first down, which moved the chains on 54 occasions … Was a perfect 3-of-3 on fourth down pass attempts (two conversions) … Didn’t reach 200-yard passing mark until Game 4 (William & Mary) after opening the season with 168 (NC State), 195 (Gardner-Webb) and a season-low 138-yard effort (Navy) … Finished the year with a run of nine-straight 200-plus outings … Prior to aforementioned notables vs. Cincinnati, SMU and Connecticut, threw for 210 vs. William & Mary, 202 vs. Old Dominion, 229 vs. Temple, 313 vs. UCF and 217 vs. USF before closing with 308 (and three TDs) vs. Tulsa … Surpassed the 40 attempt mark four times (52/Cincinnati, 50/UConn, 48/Tulsa, 42/SMU) … Completed 30 or more throws on three occasions (season-high 34 against Huskies, 32 each against Bearcats and Mustangs).2018: Experienced action in 10 games and made five starts as a true freshman … Did not play in opener vs. North Carolina A&T (coach’s decision) and in season finale at NC State (injury) … Made collegiate debut in Week 2 against North Carolina and played key role in the Pirates’ 41-19 victory at Dowdy-Ficklen Stadium … Earned first career start against league champion UCF at home and opened the four following contests (Memphis, Tulane, Connecticut, Cincinnati) … Completed 127-of-263 passes (48.3 percent) for 1,785 yards and a team-high 12 touchdowns … On the ground, topped program with 592 yards on 119 attempts (5.0 ypc) and reached paydirt on an ECU-best six occasions … Turned in the second-highest single-season rushing total among quarterbacks in school history, trailing only Leander Green (1979) by a mere 40 yards … Also stood second among all true freshmen nationally in rushing yards and finished as the Pirates’ leading ground gainer in eight outings – 48/USF, 63/Old Dominion, 56/Temple, 45/Houston, 69/UCF, 57/Memphis, 67/Tulane, 130/Connecticut … Stood 11th nationally in yards per completion (14.1), while ranking among AAC leaders in total offense (237.7 ypg), passing yards per game (178.5) and passing efficiency (118.08) … As a starter, racked up 1,985 total yards – an average of 397.0 per contest … Seventy-two (72) of 127 completions went for 10-plus yards (23 for 25-plus), which included a career-long 80-yarder to Trevon Brown at Tulane … Turned in a personal-long rush of 53 yards at Temple, which bested a 49-yarder vs. Old Dominion a week earlier … Selected as the AAC Offensive Player-of-the-Week on Nov. 19 after surpassing the century ground mark (130/1 TD) and passing for an additional 242 yards (4 TD) against the Huskies … Triple-digit rushing effort vs. UConn marked the third-highest single-game total ever recorded by an ECU starting QB in program history behind Leander Green (178/Richmond/1979) and Jeff Blake (161/Northern Illinois/1990) … Posted 27 carries in inaugural start against UCF, most by a Pirate signal-caller since 1982 (Kevin Ingram/William & Mary) … Tallied ground scores vs. North Carolina (2), USF, Old Dominion, Houston and Connecticut … Touchdown passes came against Connecticut (career-high 4), Memphis (3), Tulane (2), Houston, UCF and Cincinnati … Passed for at least 360 yards in first three career starts – 406 vs. UCF, 449 vs. Memphis (second-highest recorded by an FBS true freshman in 2018) and 360 vs. Tulane … Also completed at least one toss of 40-plus yards in games against Knights, Tigers and Green Wave … After racking up 475 total yards in initial start against UCF, accumulated 506 overall yards vs. Memphis … Also established a personal best in completions (34) vs. Tigers before establishing a career-high in attempts (67) against Tulane … Was the only FBS quarterback with multiple 60-plus attempt contests (62/Memphis, 67/Tulane) during the season … Was 13-of-28 for 144 yards and one score in first five games (coming off the bench) before assuming full-time starting role … On the flipside, scored five of six rushing TDs as a backup … Suffered only three interceptions all season (UCF, Tulane, Cincinnati) and was also charged with just three lost fumbles (2 vs. Knights, 1 vs. Bearcats) … Credited with a tackle vs. Tulane (interception return) … Averaged 6.2 yards per carry in non-conference games (16-99), compared to a 4.8 clip in eight league tilts … Tallied 14 (9 passing, 5 rushing) of 18 overall touchdowns at Dowdy-Ficklen Stadium … Averaged 328.2 passing yards per game in second half of season (last five games played) after opening season with a 28.8 ypg clip (first five appearances) … Converted four of five fourth down rushes into a first down or score … Officially enrolled early at ECU in January and participated with the Pirates in spring drills … Earned “Top Offensive Newcomer” honors at the conclusion of spring workouts after completing a combined 22-of-36 passes (61.1 percent) for 302 yards and one TD during two scrimmages.

name: Brendan Costello
hs_athletic_background: Costello led San Clemente to their league title and their first since 1999. He was named MVP and earned first-team All-CIF South Coast honors for his play. The Tritons finished 7-5 as Costello completed 167-of-264 passing for 2,328-yards with 22 touchdowns and just five interceptions. He also ran for 658-yards on 108 carries and scored nine rushing touchdowns. As a junior, he completed 168-of-262 passes for 2,132-yards with 23 touchdowns and just two interceptions. In the 2017 season he ran for 392-yards and five touchdowns on 99 carries. His sophomore season he completed 18-of-37 passing attempts for 198-yards with two touchdowns and no picks. He ran for 115-yards and a touchdown on 20 carries. His career total offense is 5,823-yards and 62 touchdowns responsible for. Costello led the Tritons to 16 wins during his two seasons as the starting quarterback, including a 9-2 record in 2017 He was named first team All-South Coast in 2017 and 2018. He was named San Clemente HS Scholar Athlete of the year in 2017 and 2018 and National Football Foundation Scholar Athlete in 2018. Costello was on the Superintendent/Principal Honor Roll in 2017 and 2018Scouting Report: Let’s start with fun to watch because he makes a lot of plays and rarely is flat or at a loss to find something. He follows in the tradition of good deep throwers for Yurcich as he can throw the deep ball down the middle or on the sidelines well. He rarely misses on intermediate or deep throws short. If he misses, he is going to be long which allows receivers to know they are going to extend to or chase down the ball. On the out route he is often high, same with middle short to intermediate. His long throws are more ropes than floaters, in other words look more like Taylor Cornelius that Mason Rudolph. His release is quick, which allows him to release the ball in a slot between ¾ and over the top. The ball does drop a little low, but he brings it so quick it never mattered. He will sometimes drop between ¾ and side when throwing on the run. He has more short throws when throwing on the run, but that is the same with most quarterbacks. Once he scrambles, it is 80 percent he is going to end up running and he has good instincts. He doesn’t run away from people but avoids them and has good moves. I saw him hurdle twice and that is impressive. You can tell by the way he plays that he has some “it’. His height did not ever bother him in the three games I watched, and I watched the playoff loss where they were pounded good by St. John Bosco. He got frustrated but who wouldn’t. He was running for his skin the whole game.
